hooks.prompt: Check if the user's Bash command is a git commit. If the command contains "git commit" (including "git commit -m", "git commit -a", etc.), block it and respond with: {"decision": "deny", "reason": "Direct git commits are blocked in this project. Use /gitcommit or @gitcommit-agent instead — it runs tests and quality checks before committing. Type /gitcommit \"your message\" to commit safely."}. If the command does NOT contain "git commit" (e.g., git status, git add, git push, git diff, git log, tsc, cargo, npm, vitest, mkdir, ls, etc.), allow it with: {"decision": "allow"}.
